title: Progress, Loader, Skeleton & Empty
intro: Four ways to fill the gap when there's nothing real to show yet: `wf-skeleton` mimics the layout that's coming, `wf-loader` spins for short indeterminate waits, `wf-progress` reports a known percent, and `wf-empty-state` says "nothing here" with a way out. Pick by what you know — the shape, the duration, the percent, or the absence (DESIGN.md 4.6).

blocks.body:
These four components answer the same question — "what do I render when the real content isn't here?" — but the right answer depends on what you know.

If you know the *shape* of what's loading (a table, a card grid, a profile header), use `wf-skeleton`: gray shimmer bars in the eventual layout, so the page doesn't reflow when data lands. If you know the *percent complete* (an upload, a batch import), use `wf-progress`. If you know neither but the wait is short, use `wf-loader` — a spinner. And if there is genuinely *no data*, use `wf-empty-state` with a CTA so the screen isn't a dead end.

All four live in `components/status/status.css` alongside `wf-status-dot` and `wf-status-indicator`. Skeleton, loader-overlay, and empty-state all respect or use the shared `wf-fade-in` / shimmer animations, and skeleton honors `prefers-reduced-motion`.

blocks.title: 4.6 — which one to reach for
blocks.intro: From DESIGN.md 4.6. The decision shortcut: known shape, loading? Skeleton. Unknown shape, short wait? Loader. Determinate progress? Progress. No data? EmptyState (with a CTA).

items.rule: Use wf-skeleton when content is loading and you can predict its shape.
items.why: Gray bars matching the eventual table rows or card grid keep perceived performance high and prevent layout shift when real data arrives.

items.rule: Use wf-loader for indeterminate, short-duration waits where you don't know the shape of what's coming.
items.why: A spinner says "working" without promising a layout. Don't spin for more than a few seconds — beyond that, switch to Progress.

items.rule: Use wf-progress for determinate operations where you can show percent complete — file upload, batch reconciliation, multi-step import.
items.why: A bar that crawls from 0 to 100% sets expectations a spinner can't; long waits need a sense of how long.

items.rule: Use wf-empty-state for "there's no data here yet," and always pair it with a primary action.
items.why: An "Add your first contract" button turns a dead end into a next step.

blocks.title: Skeleton — card placeholder
blocks.caption: A card-shaped skeleton: image block over heading and text bars. Use when the layout is known and content is loading in place — the shimmer reserves the exact space the real card will occupy.

blocks.title: Skeleton shapes
blocks.intro: Every skeleton starts with the base `wf-skeleton` class (the shimmering gradient) plus one shape modifier. Compose them inside a `wf-skeleton-card`, `wf-skeleton-table-row`, or `wf-skeleton-list-item` container to mirror the eventual layout.

rows.name: wf-skeleton-text
rows.when: A single line of body copy (16px tall). Add wf-skeleton-text-short for 60% width or wf-skeleton-text-xs for 30%.

rows.name: wf-skeleton-heading
rows.when: A title bar — taller (24px) and half-width. wf-skeleton-heading-lg is 32px / 40%.

rows.name: wf-skeleton-rect
rows.when: An image or media block; set explicit width/height inline.

rows.name: wf-skeleton-avatar
rows.when: A round avatar. -sm is 32px, base is 40px, -lg is 56px; wf-skeleton-circle takes a custom size.

rows.name: wf-skeleton-card
rows.when: A pre-composed card shell — pairs with wf-skeleton-card-image (150px) and wf-skeleton-card-body.

rows.name: wf-skeleton-table-row
rows.when: A loading table row — flexed columns; first child auto-rounds into an avatar circle.

blocks.title: Loader — spinner with text
blocks.caption: An indeterminate spinner with a label. Use for blocking waits of unknown duration — but if it spins longer than a few seconds, you wanted Progress instead.

blocks.title: Loader modifiers
blocks.intro: A `wf-loader` wraps a `wf-loader-spinner` and an optional `wf-loader-text`. Default layout is vertical (spinner over text); modifiers change size, color, direction, and placement.

rows.name: wf-loader (base)
rows.when: Default medium (24px) spinner, primary blue, vertical stack.

rows.name: wf-loader-horizontal
rows.when: Spinner and text side by side instead of stacked.

rows.name: wf-loader-sm / wf-loader-lg
rows.when: 16px (sm) or 40px (lg) spinner; text scales to match.

rows.name: wf-loader-success / -warning / -error
rows.when: Recolors the spinner's top arc to the semantic color. Rare — most loaders stay primary.

rows.name: wf-loader-inverse
rows.when: White spinner for dark backgrounds and overlays.

rows.name: wf-loader-inline
rows.when: Drop a spinner mid-sentence; pairs with wf-loader-sm + wf-loader-horizontal.
rows.code: Saving changes

rows.name: wf-loader-overlay
rows.when: Fullscreen blocking wait — fixed, dimmed scrim, centered loader card.
rows.code: Loading data...

blocks.title: Progress — color variants
blocks.caption: Determinate bars in default, success, warning, and danger. Use only when percent-complete is genuinely known; drive the bar by setting wf-progress-fill's inline width.

blocks.title: Progress modifiers
blocks.intro: A `wf-progress` holds an optional `wf-progress-label` (description + percent) and a `wf-progress-bar` with a `wf-progress-fill`. Percent is set by the fill's inline `width`. Modifiers go on the outer `wf-progress`.

rows.name: wf-progress (base)
rows.when: Labeled medium (8px) primary bar; set width on the fill.
rows.code: Volume Fulfilled75%

rows.name: wf-progress-success / -warning / -error
rows.when: Recolors the fill green / amber / red. error is aliased "Error / Danger".

rows.name: wf-progress-sm / wf-progress-lg
rows.when: Bar height 4px (sm) or 12px (lg); default is 8px.

rows.name: wf-progress-bar-only
rows.when: Hides the label row entirely — just the bar (e.g. inside a file-upload card).

rows.name: wf-progress-indeterminate
rows.when: When the percent is genuinely unknown but you still want a bar — forces a 30% sliver that sweeps across. (Usually prefer a loader here.)

blocks.title: EmptyState — variants
blocks.caption: No-results, error, success, and warning treatments — each an icon, title, description, and action. Use when there is genuinely nothing to show (DESIGN.md 4.6), and pair with a CTA.

blocks.title: EmptyState anatomy & variants
blocks.intro: A `wf-empty-state` centers a `wf-empty-state-icon` (emoji or `wf-empty-state-icon-container` for an SVG), a `wf-empty-state-title`, a `wf-empty-state-description`, and one or more actions. Semantic and sizing modifiers go on the outer element.

rows.name: wf-empty-state (base)
rows.when: The default "no data yet" state — icon, title, description, primary CTA.
rows.code:
📋

No contracts found

Create your first contract to get started.

rows.name: wf-empty-state-search
rows.when: No results from a search/filter — neutral icon, no CTA (the fix is changing the query).
rows.code:
🔍

No results found

Try adjusting your search or filter criteria.

rows.name: wf-empty-state-error
rows.when: A load failure — recolors the icon and title to danger; pair with a Retry.
rows.code:
⚠️

Unable to load data

rows.name: wf-empty-state-success / -warning
rows.when: "All caught up" (success, green icon) or "action required" (warning, amber icon).
rows.code:
✅

All caught up!

rows.name: wf-empty-state-actions
rows.when: Two or more buttons — wraps and centers them with consistent gap.

rows.name: wf-empty-state-compact / -inline
rows.when: Compact shrinks to 120px min-height for sidebars/cards; inline drops the min-height for tables.
rows.code:
🔔

No notifications

rows.name: wf-empty-state-bordered
rows.when: Drop zones — a dashed border and tinted background that read as "put something here."
rows.code:
📤

Drop files here

blocks.title: Status tokens
blocks.intro: Status-specific tokens declared at the top of status.css, plus the shared color/radius tokens these components resolve against. Hex values shown are the in-file fallbacks.

rows.use: Progress bar thickness; md is the default.
Spinner diameter; md is the default.
The resting gray of a skeleton bar (and the still color under reduced-motion).
The lighter band that sweeps across in the shimmer gradient.
Default emoji icon size; compact drops it to 32px.
Default progress fill and spinner top-arc color.
The success / warning / error variant colors across all four components.

items.title: Progress percent is inline width, not a class
items.detail: There's no wf-progress-75 modifier — you set width on the .wf-progress-fill element directly (style="width: 75%"). The fill animates from 0 via the wf-progress-fill keyframe and eases width changes over 200ms.

items.title: Indeterminate progress hard-locks the width
items.detail: .wf-progress-indeterminate .wf-progress-fill sets width: 30% !important and sweeps via translateX. Any inline width you set is ignored — that's intentional, but it means you can't half-convert a determinate bar.

items.title: The first cell of a skeleton table row auto-becomes a circle
items.detail: .wf-skeleton-table-row .wf-skeleton:first-child is forced to flex: 0 0 40px and full radius — it's treated as an avatar slot. If your first column isn't an avatar, restructure the row rather than fighting the selector.

items.title: Skeleton card image keeps square corners
items.detail: .wf-skeleton-card-image is border-radius: 0 so it sits flush against the rounded card top. If you build a card by hand with wf-skeleton-rect instead, add border-radius: 0 yourself or the corners won't line up.

items.title: Search empty state has no CTA on purpose
items.detail: DESIGN.md 4.6 says always pair EmptyState with an action — but wf-empty-state-search omits it because the user's action is editing the query, not clicking a button. Don't bolt a CTA onto a no-results state.

items.title: Reduced motion stops skeletons but not spinners
items.detail: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) flattens wf-skeleton to a static gray, but the loader spinner and progress sweep keep animating. If full motion-reduction matters for a prototype, disable those yourself.
